title: David Petraeus on Office Hours
summary: U.S. Army General (ret.) and former CIA Director David Petraeus speaks with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) about ISIS, America’s other challenges in the world, how he gets his news, and his ideal vacation.
body: U.S. Army General (ret.) and former CIA Director David Petraeus speaks with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) about ISIS, America’s other challenges in the world, how he gets his news, and his ideal vacation. The latest from Belfer See more Gallery 1 of 4 David Petraeus: My Daily News Regimen 2 of 4 David Petraeus: Applying PhD Training to the Battlefield 3 of 4 David Petraeus: His Ideal Vacation 4 of 4 David Petraeus: The Ultramarathon Approach to Defeating ISIS David H. Petraeus is a retired four-star Army general and former director of the Central Intelligence Agency. Currently, he is a non-resident senior fellow at Harvard Kennedy School’s Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs where he is working with Center Director Graham Allison to explore renewed U.S. and North American competitiveness. Their work focuses on major technological, scientific and economic dynamics that are spurring renewed U.S. and North American competitiveness, exploring in particular the impact of the ongoing revolutions in energy, information technology, advanced manufacturing, and life sciences. Petraeus spent 37 years in the Army, including roles as commander of U.S. and international military forces in Afghanistan, commander of U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM), and commanding general of the multi-national force in Iraq. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: In an interview, retired U.S. Army General and former CIA Director David Petraeus discusses a range of topics including the ongoing fight against ISIS, other global challenges facing America, his personal news consumption habits, and his ideal vacation. The conversation is part of the Belfer Center's 'Office Hours' series. Petraeus, currently a non-resident senior fellow at the Harvard Kennedy School's Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, is collaborating with Center Director Graham Allison on a project examining renewed U.S. and North American competitiveness. Their research focuses on the transformative impact of key technological and scientific revolutions in energy, information technology, advanced manufacturing, and life sciences. Petraeus draws on his extensive 37-year military career, which included commanding roles in Afghanistan, at U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM), and as the commanding general of the multi-national force in Iraq.
result.main_content_zh: 在一场访谈中，已退休的美国陆军上将、前中央情报局局长戴维·彼得雷乌斯讨论了包括打击伊斯兰国（ISIS）、美国面临的其他全球挑战、个人获取新闻的习惯以及理想的度假方式等一系列话题。此次对话是贝尔弗科学与国际事务中心“Office Hours”系列的一部分。彼得雷乌斯目前是哈佛大学肯尼迪政府学院贝尔弗中心的非常驻高级研究员，正与中心主任格雷厄姆·艾利森合作一个研究项目，探讨美国和北美重振竞争力的问题。他们的研究重点集中在能源、信息技术、先进制造业和生命科学等领域的关键技术与科学革命所带来的变革性影响。彼得雷乌斯借鉴了他长达37年的广泛军事生涯，其履历包括在阿富汗的指挥职务、担任美国中央司令部司令以及伊拉克多国部队指挥官等。
